must_detect.matched_text: Public, already-announced financing facts (e.g., the Meadowline REIT prospectus) are outside MNPI scope; buzzwords and place metaphors like “playing on the front foot” do not form part of any data set.

MNPI context: monitoring of wall-crossing, insider lists, and deal-room access for a UK issuer listed on the fictional Britannic Securities Exchange is risk-based and not influenced by product slogans or sports banter about West Dock Rovers.
Product and service references (system-level only): Nimbus ISA, Cedarline Pension, and Harrier Derivatives may appear in audit logs to tag platforms, not to identify natural persons.
Special-category data: The Data Subject (Employee ID ZX-402 at Ardenfold Finance plc) recorded voluntary trade union membership with the Aurora Workers Union for payroll deduction and staff forum eligibility.
We do not collect biometric or genetic identifiers, nor do we profile sex life, sexual orientation, religion, or political opinions; forms with those labels are disabled by default (e.g., “Religion: —”, “NI Number: [not collected via DSAR portal]”).
Lawful bases: contract (employment), legal obligation (regulatory reporting), legitimate interests (security), and tasks in the public interest supporting market-abuse prevention consistent with UK MAR.
Retention: insider-list entries 7 years; CCTV 30 days; access logs 12 months; HR files 6 years post-employment; Nimbus ISA marketing events aggregated within 90 days without inclusion of PII.
DSAR workflow: submit via dataprotection@ardenfoldfinance.example; we may verify identity using Employee ID only and will redact deal notes containing non-personal codewords such as Blue Lantern Code and Sandbar Playbook.
International transfers: limited, pseudonymised monitoring to North Quay Operations Hub (UK) and Fenreach Service Centre (EEA) under the UK International Data Transfer Agreement and the Inclusive Horizons Policy.
We champion inclusion and wellbeing through the Inclusive Horizons Policy—statements like “bring your whole self to work” and “wellness Wednesdays” are cultural, not data fields.
Public, already-announced financing facts (e.g., the Meadowline REIT prospectus) are outside MNPI scope; buzzwords and place metaphors like “playing on the front foot” do not form part of any data set.
